title: revocation + advisory feed format
manager: A revocation mechanism and an advisory feed — so when a checker or runtime turns out to have a vulnerability, the feed identifies the whole blast radius of certificates that need re-certification. Verification you can't revoke is verification you can't trust after a bug is found; the advisory feed (shaped like RustSec or GHSA) is how a checker CVE propagates to "these proofs are now suspect and must be re-run."
cs: revocation + advisory feed (RustSec/GHSA-shaped), keyed by checker/runtime version; identifies the re-certification blast radius of a checker CVE.
